title: Papaver calciphilum
intro: Papaver calciphilum, also known as the calciphilous poppy, is a perennial plant belonging to the Papaveraceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Papaver calciphilum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Papaver calciphilum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, migraines, and anxiety.
taxonomy: Papaver calciphilum is a species of poppy in the family Papaveraceae. It is native to Europe, and its classification is as follows: it is a genus of Papaver, with the specific epithet calciphilum indicating its preference for calcareous soils. The species is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the order Papaverales. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Papaver calciphilum's classification provides valuable information about its habits, habitats, and potential uses.
